title: $language
text: $language Return or set the current language code used for language-related global objects and language setups. $language $language = Language Example: $language = "FR" Parameters Table 1. Parameters Parameter Data Type Description Language String Name of the desired language. It is recommended that you use the country codes from the ISO or ANSI standards. Return Values Current language code. Use Allowed in Form, Report, and Service components, but not in self-contained components. Description Use $language to set or return the name of the current language. If the language has not been set in an assignment file (with the setting $LANGUAGE in USYS:usys.asn or your local assignment file), it defaults to USA. If you explicitly define a value for $language , the assignment file setting is ignored. Example: Setting $language The following example saves the current language in general variable $1, and sets the current language to German, which has a country code of DE: ; save current language ; set current language to German $1 = $language $language = "DE" Copy Example: Setting $language and $variation The following example shows how to correctly set $language and $variation . The example assumes that you do not want to override any assignment file settings, but do want to change the language and library if the defaults have been given. ; trigger: apStart if ($language = "USA") ; default language used, so change to UK $language = "UK" endif ; otherwise, leave alone if ($variation = "USYS") ; default library used, so change to TECH_PUBS $variation = "TECH_PUBS" endif ; otherwise, leave alone Copy The only problem with the example (and the problem is unavoidable) is that the assignment file below is ignored: $LANGUAGE = USA $VARIATION = USYS Copy Related Links language Specify the language used by the Sybase SQL Server for error messages. display Presents the form on the screen as display-only. $keyboard Return or set the current keyboard translation table. $variation Return or specify the name of the library containing objects needed by the application or component. trigger apStart System trigger that starts the application environment and controls the interaction of the application with the component manager. $display Return the name of the current display device translation table. $LANGUAGE Specify the language to use for global objects and language setups.
